concept_name: Magnetic Force on a Current-Carrying Wire (F = I L × B)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.36 (force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Concept A15, the natural successor to magnetic_force_moving_charge (the prerequisite): a wire is just a pipe full of moving charges, so the single-charge force F = qv×B summed over all the carriers in a length L becomes F = I L × B. The atomic idea being taught is (1) the DERIVATION as a picture — n carriers per unit volume, each drifting at v_d, the per-carrier force qv_dB adds up to (nAL)·q·v_d·B = (I)(LB), so the messy microscopic picture collapses into one clean macroscopic law; (2) DIRECTION via the right-hand rule on the macroscopic vectors L (along conventional current) and B; (3) the sin θ between L and B — the wire-orientation analogue of the v–B angle; (4) two classic traps — the 30° decoy angle (θ is between L and B, not between the wire and the page edge) and the bent-wire result (only the straight chord from end to end matters, F = I (L_chord) × B); (5) the closed-loop net-zero result that seeds the next concept (torque_on_current_loop_in_field). Indian anchor: the electric motor in every Indian home — the ceiling fan, the mixer-grinder, the borewell pump — every one of them spins because F = I L × B pushes on current-carrying wires sitting in a magnetic field.

real_world_anchor.primary: Look up at the ceiling fan spinning over your head right now. Inside its motor, coils of copper wire carry current, and they sit inside the magnetic field of fixed magnets. The field pushes on every current-carrying wire with a force F = I L × B, and those pushes add up into the turning that spins the blades. The same force runs the mixer-grinder in the kitchen and the borewell pump that lifts water — every electric motor in an Indian home is F = I L × B at work.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the loudspeaker in your phone or in a cheap Bluetooth speaker. A coil of wire is glued to the paper cone and sits in the gap of a small permanent magnet. When the music signal sends current back and forth through the coil, F = I L × B pushes the coil — and the cone — in and out hundreds of times a second. That push is the sound you hear. The coil is a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field; the force is exactly the one in this lesson.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture an electric local train or the Delhi Metro pulling out of a station. Its traction motors are huge versions of the ceiling-fan motor — thick current-carrying windings sitting in a strong magnetic field. F = I L × B on those windings is what accelerates a hundred-tonne train. Scale up the current and the field, and the same little formula moves a whole train.

L.name: Length of wire inside the field

B.name: Magnitude of the uniform magnetic field

current_dir.name: Direction of conventional current along the wire

n.name: Carrier number density (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
n.derived: carrier density of copper, ~8.5e28; used only in the STATE_2 derivation picture

A.name: Wire cross-sectional area (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
A.derived: cross-section of the wire; cancels in I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

q.name: Charge per carrier (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
q.derived: electron charge magnitude; folds into I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

v_d.name: Drift velocity of carriers (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
v_d.derived: carrier drift speed; folds into I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

formulas.force_vector: F = I (L × B) — L is a vector of magnitude (wire length) pointing along the conventional current; the cross product gives the direction (right-hand rule).
formulas.force_magnitude: F = B I L sin θ — maximum when L ⊥ B (θ = 90°), zero when L ∥ B (θ = 0° or 180°).
formulas.derivation_from_single_charge: Each carrier feels q v_d B. A length L of wire holds nAL carriers. Total F = (nAL)(q v_d B) = (n A q v_d)(L B) = I L B, since I = n A q v_d.
formulas.bent_wire_chord_rule: For a wire bent between two end points, only the straight chord from start to end matters: F = I (L_chord × B). The bends cancel.
formulas.closed_loop_net_force: For a closed loop in a uniform field, the chord from start to end is zero, so the net force is zero — but opposite sides feel opposite forces, which produces a torque (next concept).

constraints: B must be uniform across the wire — a non-uniform field changes the force along the length.
L is a vector along the conventional current direction; reversing the current reverses F.
θ is the angle between L (the current direction) and B — NOT the angle the wire makes with the page edge or any drawn reference line.
When L ∥ B (θ = 0°), the cross product is zero — the wire feels no force however large the current.
For a bent or curved wire in a uniform field, F depends only on the straight chord joining the two ends: F = I (L_chord × B).
For a closed loop in a uniform field the net force is exactly zero (the chord closes on itself); the loop feels a torque instead.

aha_moment.statement: A wire is just moving charges; summing qv×B over them gives F=ILB.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: In STATE_2 the nAL carriers each carry a tiny qv_dB arrow; they stack into one big F arrow, and the symbols collapse: nAqv_d is exactly the current I, leaving F = I L B.

STATE_1.title: Hook: the ceiling fan spins because a magnetic field pushes its wires

scene_composition.text: Why does an electric motor turn?

Blue arrows = uniform field B.
Orange wire carries current I.
L points along the current.

The field pushes on the
current-carrying wire.
That push is F = I L × B.
Ceiling fan, mixer, pump — every motor in your home runs on this one push.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look up at the ceiling fan turning over your head. Why does it spin?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपने सिर के ऊपर घूमता ceiling fan देखिए। यह क्यों spin करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ తల పైన తిరుగుతున్న ceiling fan చూడండి. అది ఎందుకు spin అవుతుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Inside its motor, copper wires carry current and sit inside a magnetic field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसके motor के अंदर, copper wires current ले जाती हैं और एक magnetic field के भीतर रहती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని motor లోపల, copper wires current తీసుకెళ్తాయి మరియు ఒక magnetic field లో ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The field pushes on each current-carrying wire. That push is the force we will build today.
tts_sentences.text_hi: field हर current-carrying wire पर push करती है। वही push है वह force जिसे हम आज समझेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: field ప్రతి current-carrying wire ని push చేస్తుంది. ఆ push నే మనం ఈరోజు నేర్చుకోబోయే force.

tts_sentences.text_en: The blue arrows are the field B. The orange wire carries current I, and L points along the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नीले arrows field B हैं। नारंगी wire current I ले जाती है, और L current की direction में point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: నీలి arrows field B అవుతాయి. నారింజ wire current I ని తీసుకెళ్తుంది, మరియు L current వెంట point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The whole formula is F equals I times L cross B. Let us see where it comes from.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पूरा formula है F equals I times L cross B। आइए देखें यह कहाँ से आता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొత్తం formula ఏమిటంటే F equals I times L cross B. ఇది ఎక్కడ నుండి వస్తుందో చూద్దాం.

STATE_2.title: Derivation as a picture: add up qv×B over every carrier → F = I L × B

scene_composition.text: Where does F = I L × B come from?

A wire is a pipe of moving charges.
Each carrier feels q v_d B.
A length L holds n·A·L carriers.

F = (n·A·L)·(q·v_d·B)
     = (n·A·q·v_d)·(L·B)
and n·A·q·v_d is just I.
So F = I L B. The messy carrier picture collapses into one clean law. A wire is just charges — qv×B, added up.

tts_sentences.text_en: You already know the force on one moving charge: q times v cross B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप पहले से जानते हैं एक moving charge पर force: q times v cross B।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు ఇప్పటికే ఒక moving charge పై force తెలుసు: q times v cross B.

tts_sentences.text_en: A wire is nothing but a pipe full of moving charges, all drifting along the wire at the drift speed v d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक wire और कुछ नहीं बल्कि moving charges से भरा एक pipe है, सब drift speed v d पर wire के साथ drift कर रहे हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక wire అంటే moving charges తో నిండిన ఒక pipe మాత్రమే, అన్నీ drift speed v d తో wire వెంట drift చేస్తున్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. If one charge feels q v d B, what does a whole length L of wire feel? Think before we add them up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करिए। अगर एक charge q v d B feel करता है, तो wire की पूरी length L क्या feel करेगी? हम add करने से पहले सोचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి. ఒక charge q v d B feel చేస్తే, మొత్తం length L wire ఏమి feel చేస్తుంది? మనం add చేయడానికి ముందు ఆలోచించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Count the carriers. A length L of wire with cross-section A holds n times A times L of them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: carriers count करिए। cross-section A वाली wire की length L में n times A times L carriers होती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: carriers లెక్కించండి. cross-section A ఉన్న wire లో length L లో n times A times L carriers ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each one feels q v d B, so the total force is n A L times q v d B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर एक q v d B feel करती है, इसलिए total force है n A L times q v d B।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి ఒక్కటి q v d B feel చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి total force n A L times q v d B అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now regroup the symbols. Pull out n times A times q times v d, and leave L times B behind.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब symbols को regroup करिए। n times A times q times v d बाहर निकालिए, और L times B छोड़ दीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు symbols ని regroup చేయండి. n times A times q times v d బయటకు తీయండి, మరియు L times B వదిలిపెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: But n times A times q times v d is exactly the current I. That is the definition of current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन n times A times q times v d बिल्कुल current I है। यही current की definition है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ n times A times q times v d సరిగ్గా current I అవుతుంది. ఇదే current యొక్క definition.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the whole thing collapses to F equals I L B. The messy carrier picture becomes one clean law.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो पूरी चीज़ F equals I L B में collapse हो जाती है। जटिल carrier picture एक clean law बन जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి మొత్తం విషయం F equals I L B గా collapse అవుతుంది. సంకీర్ణమైన carrier picture ఒక clean law గా మారుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is the big idea. A wire is just charges, and I L cross B is q v cross B added up over all of them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही बड़ा idea है। एक wire बस charges है, और I L cross B वही q v cross B है जो उन सभी पर add हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే పెద్ద idea. ఒక wire కేవలం charges మాత్రమే, మరియు I L cross B అంటే q v cross B వాటన్నింటి మీద కలిపినది.

STATE_3.title: Direction: the right-hand rule on L and B

scene_composition.text: Which way does the force point?

Right hand:
fingers point along L (the current),
curl toward B,
thumb gives F.

F is perpendicular to BOTH
the wire and the field.
Reverse the current → F flips.
Same right-hand rule as the single charge — now L plays the role v played. Flip the current direction and the whole force reverses.

tts_sentences.text_en: We have the size of the force. Now, which way does it push the wire?
tts_sentences.text_hi: हमारे पास force का size है। अब, यह wire को किस direction में push करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మనకు force యొక్క పరిమాణం తెలిసింది. ఇప్పుడు, ఇది wire ని ఏ direction లో push చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the right hand. Point your fingers along L, the current direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: right hand इस्तेमाल करिए। अपनी उंगलियाँ L की तरफ point करिए, current direction में।
tts_sentences.text_te: right hand వాడండి. మీ వేళ్ళను L వైపు point చేయండి, current direction లో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Curl them toward B, the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उन्हें B, यानी field की तरफ curl करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటిని B, అంటే field వైపు curl చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your thumb now points along F — the force on the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपका thumb अब F के साथ point करता है — wire पर force।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ thumb ఇప్పుడు F వెంట point చేస్తుంది — wire పై force.

tts_sentences.text_en: F is perpendicular to both the wire and the field — exactly like the single-charge rule, with L playing the part v played.
tts_sentences.text_hi: F wire और field दोनों के perpendicular है — बिल्कुल single-charge rule की तरह, जिसमें L वह भूमिका निभाता है जो v निभाती थी।
tts_sentences.text_te: F wire మరియు field రెండింటికీ perpendicular గా ఉంటుంది — single-charge rule లాగే, L అక్కడ v పాత్ర పోషిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now flip the current the other way. Watch the force flip with it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब current को दूसरी दिशा में flip करिए। देखिए force भी इसके साथ flip होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు current ని వేరే వైపు flip చేయండి. force కూడా దానితో పాటు flip అవడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Reverse the current, reverse the force. That sign change is how a motor keeps turning the same way.
tts_sentences.text_hi: current reverse करो, force reverse होगी। वही sign change है जिससे motor एक ही direction में घूमती रहती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: current reverse చేయండి, force reverse అవుతుంది. ఆ sign change వల్లనే motor ఒకే direction లో తిరుగుతూ ఉంటుంది.

STATE_4.title: The angle trap: θ is between L and B, not the wire and the page

scene_composition.text: What is the angle θ in F = B I L sin θ?

Tempting wrong answer: 30°,
the angle the wire makes
with the edge of the page.

True θ = angle between L and B.
Here L ⊥ B, so θ = 90°
and F = B I L (maximum).
Always read θ between the current direction L and the field B — never between the wire and some drawn line. Tilt L toward B and F shrinks as sin θ.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnitude is B times I times L times sine theta. Everything hangs on which angle theta is.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnitude है B times I times L times sine theta। सब कुछ इस पर निर्भर है कि angle theta कौन सा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnitude అనేది B times I times L times sine theta. అన్నీ ఏ angle theta అనే దానిపై ఆధారపడి ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. The wire is drawn at thirty degrees to the page edge. Is theta thirty degrees? Decide before I show you.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करिए। wire को page edge से thirty degrees पर draw किया गया है। क्या theta thirty degrees है? मैं दिखाने से पहले तय करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి. wire page edge కి thirty degrees వద్ద draw చేయబడింది. theta thirty degrees అవుతుందా? నేను చూపించే ముందే నిర్ణయించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It is a trap. The thirty-degree angle to the page edge has nothing to do with the physics.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक trap है। page edge से thirty-degree angle का physics से कोई लेना-देना नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక trap. page edge కి thirty-degree angle physics తో ఎటువంటి సంబంధం లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Theta is the angle between L, the current direction, and B, the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta, L यानी current direction और B यानी field के बीच का angle है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta అనేది L అంటే current direction మరియు B అంటే field మధ్య ఉన్న angle.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here L points across the field, so theta is ninety degrees, and the force is at its maximum, B I L.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ L field के आर-पार point करता है, इसलिए theta ninety degrees है, और force अपने maximum B I L पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ L field అడ్డంగా point చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి theta ninety degrees, మరియు force దాని maximum B I L వద్ద ఉంటుంది.
tts_sentences.math_show: F = B I L\,\sin 90^{\circ} = B I L

tts_sentences.text_en: Tilt L toward B and theta shrinks. The force falls off as sine theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: L को B की तरफ tilt करिए और theta कम होता जाएगा। force sine theta के अनुसार कम होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: L ని B వైపు tilt చేయండి మరియు theta తగ్గిపోతుంది. force sine theta తో తగ్గిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: When L lines up with B, theta is zero, sine zero is zero, and the wire feels no force at all.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जब L, B के साथ line up हो जाए, theta zero होता है, sine zero भी zero है, और wire को बिल्कुल force नहीं लगती।
tts_sentences.text_te: L, B తో line up అయినప్పుడు, theta zero అవుతుంది, sine zero కూడా zero, మరియు wire కి అస్సలు force అనుభవం కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: θ in F = BIL sin θ is the angle the wire makes with the edge of the page or with the horizontal.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: θ is ALWAYS the angle between the current direction L and the field B. The angle to any drawn line on the page is irrelevant.

STATE_5.title: Bent wire = its straight chord: F = I (L_chord × B)

scene_composition.text: A bent wire — does every kink matter?

The wire zig-zags between two ends.
It looks complicated.
Do we add up every segment?

No — only the straight CHORD
from one end to the other counts.
F = I (L_chord × B).
In a uniform field the bends cancel; the force depends only on the straight line joining the two ends. Replace any bent wire with its chord.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a wire that zig-zags between two end points. It carries the same current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक wire है जो दो end points के बीच zig-zag करती है। यह वही current I ले जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ రెండు end points మధ్య zig-zag చేసే ఒక wire ఉంది. ఇది అదే current I తీసుకెళ్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. To get the force, do we have to add up every twist and bend? Decide before we reveal it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करिए। force पाने के लिए, क्या हमें हर twist और bend को add करना होगा? हम reveal करने से पहले तय करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి. force కనుగొనడానికి, ప్రతి twist మరియు bend ని add చేయాలా? మనం reveal చేయడానికి ముందు నిర్ణయించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The surprising answer is no. In a uniform field, all the bends cancel out.
tts_sentences.text_hi: चौंकाने वाला जवाब है नहीं। uniform field में, सारे bends cancel out हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆశ్చర్యకరమైన జవాబు — లేదు అని. uniform field లో, అన్ని bends cancel out అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only the straight chord from one end to the other matters.
tts_sentences.text_hi: केवल एक end से दूसरे end तक straight chord ही matter करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక end నుండి మరొక end వరకు straight chord మాత్రమే matter చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — replace the whole bent wire with that single straight chord.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — पूरी bent wire को उस single straight chord से replace करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — మొత్తం bent wire ని ఆ single straight chord తో replace చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The force is I times that chord vector cross B. The wiggles in between contribute nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: force है I times वह chord vector cross B। बीच में जो wiggles हैं वे कुछ contribute नहीं करते।
tts_sentences.text_te: force అంటే I times ఆ chord vector cross B. మధ్యలో ఉన్న wiggles ఏమీ contribute చేయవు.

tts_sentences.text_en: So for any bent or curved wire in a uniform field, just draw the straight line joining its two ends.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो uniform field में किसी भी bent या curved wire के लिए, बस उसके दोनों ends को जोड़ने वाली straight line खींचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి uniform field లో ఏ bent లేదా curved wire కైనా, కేవలం దాని రెండు ends ను కలిపే straight line గీయండి.

misconception_watch.belief: For a bent wire you must integrate the force over every little segment and add them all up.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a UNIFORM field only the straight chord between the two end points matters: F = I (L_chord × B). The bends cancel exactly.

STATE_6.title: A closed loop feels zero net force (but a torque) — the chord closes

scene_composition.text: What about a closed loop?

Opposite sides carry current
in opposite directions,
so their forces point opposite ways.

The chord from start back to start
is zero → net force = 0.
The forces TWIST it instead.
A closed loop's chord closes on itself, so the net force is exactly zero — but the opposite forces form a couple that turns the loop. That torque is the next concept.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now bend the wire all the way into a closed square loop carrying current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire को पूरी तरह एक बंद square loop में मोड़िए जो current I ले जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire ని పూర్తిగా current I తీసుకెళ్ళే ఒక closed square loop లో వంచండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. Using the chord rule, what is the net force on a closed loop? Think before we check.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करिए। chord rule का उपयोग करते हुए, closed loop पर net force क्या होगी? हम check करने से पहले सोचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి. chord rule వాడి, closed loop పై net force ఏమి అవుతుంది? మనం check చేయడానికి ముందు ఆలోచించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the chord rule. The chord runs from the starting point back to the same starting point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: chord rule इस्तेमाल करिए। chord starting point से उसी starting point तक वापस जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: chord rule వాడండి. chord starting point నుండి అదే starting point వరకు తిరిగి వెళ్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That chord has zero length, so the net force on a closed loop in a uniform field is exactly zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उस chord की length zero है, इसलिए uniform field में closed loop पर net force बिल्कुल zero है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ chord యొక్క length zero, కాబట్టి uniform field లో closed loop పై net force సరిగ్గా zero అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: But look at the sides. Opposite sides carry current opposite ways, so they feel forces in opposite directions.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन sides देखिए। Opposite sides विपरीत directions में current ले जाती हैं, इसलिए वे opposite directions में forces feel करती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ sides చూడండి. Opposite sides వ్యతిరేక directions లో current తీసుకెళ్తాయి, కాబట్టి అవి opposite directions లో forces అనుభవిస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Equal and opposite forces on opposite sides do not push the loop — they twist it. That twist is a torque.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Opposite sides पर equal और opposite forces loop को push नहीं करतीं — वे इसे twist करती हैं। वही twist है torque।
tts_sentences.text_te: Opposite sides పై equal మరియు opposite forces loop ని push చేయవు — అవి దాన్ని twist చేస్తాయి. ఆ twist నే torque అంటారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero net force, but a turning torque. That torque is exactly how a motor loop spins — and it is our next concept.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Zero net force, लेकिन एक turning torque। वही torque है जिससे motor loop spin होती है — और यह हमारा अगला concept है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Zero net force, కానీ ఒక turning torque. ఆ torque వల్లనే motor loop spin అవుతుంది — మరియు ఇది మన తదుపరి concept.

misconception_watch.belief: A current loop in a magnetic field gets pushed along by a net force.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a uniform field the net force on a closed loop is zero (the chord closes). Opposite sides feel opposite forces, which produce a TORQUE, not a push.

STATE_7.title: Try it — drag the sliders, flip the current, watch F

scene_composition.text: You drive it now

Top-right sliders: I · L · B · θ
plus a button to flip the current.
The F readout updates live.

Try: θ = 90 → biggest force
θ = 0 → no force
Flip current → F reverses
Double I → F doubles
Same right-hand rule everywhere. F = B I L sin θ — current, length, field, and the L–B angle are the four knobs.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. The top-right sliders set the current I, the length L, the field B, and the angle theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपकी बारी। Top-right sliders current I, length L, field B, और angle theta set करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ వంతు. Top-right sliders current I, length L, field B, మరియు angle theta set చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Set theta to ninety degrees and the force is at its largest, B I L.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta को ninety degrees पर set करिए और force अपने maximum B I L पर होगी।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta ని ninety degrees కి set చేయండి మరియు force దాని maximum B I L వద్ద ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag theta down to zero and the force vanishes — the wire is now along the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta को zero तक drag करिए और force गायब हो जाएगी — wire अब field के साथ है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta ని zero వరకు drag చేయండి మరియు force అదృశ్యమవుతుంది — wire ఇప్పుడు field వెంట ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Double the current and watch the force double. F is straight-line proportional to I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current को double करिए और force को double होते देखिए। F, I के straight-line proportional है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current ని double చేయండి మరియు force double అవడం చూడండి. F, I కి straight-line proportional గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Tap the flip-current button and watch the force reverse. That is the whole concept in your hands.
tts_sentences.text_hi: flip-current button tap करिए और force को reverse होते देखिए। पूरा concept आपके हाथों में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: flip-current button tap చేయండి మరియు force reverse అవడం చూడండి. మొత్తం concept మీ చేతుల్లో ఉంది.

STATE_1.label: Hook — current-carrying wire in a uniform field B, F = I L × B

STATE_2.label: Derivation as a picture — n·A·L carriers, each q·v_d·B, stacking into I·L·B
STATE_2.formula_overlay:
F = (nAL)(q v_d B)
  = (n A q v_d)(L B)
  = I L B

STATE_3.label: Direction — right-hand rule on L and B; flip current → F flips

STATE_4.label: Angle trap — θ is ∠(L,B) = 90° here, not the 30° to the page edge

STATE_5.label: Bent wire = straight chord — F = I (L_chord × B); snapshot swap (no morph)

STATE_6.label: Closed loop — chord closes → net F = 0; opposite sides → torque

STATE_7.label: Interactive — sliders for I, L, B, θ + flip-current button
STATE_7.formula_overlay:
F = B I L sin θ
F_max = B I L
flip current → F reverses
